description: Cron registry for the CONTENT_PIPELINE primary agent. Recreated on every session restart by the startup routine in CLAUDE.md. All times are local system time (Europe/Berlin).

crons.name: Morning summary across R55/R57/R61
crons.prompt:
Send the daily 8am summary to Telegram. Steps:
1. Read obsidian-brain/_index.md for current pipeline status.
2. Query Airtable base appC3HqG42ftswOvw — count records by Status in R57 table tblnpiwNYF3zJXm9Q (today's scheduled images) and R61 table tbl1hd8yprLTZia4c (today's scheduled videos).
3. Check shared/gates/pending.json for any unresolved R61 human gates.
4. Send a Telegram message with this format:
   *Daily summary — <date>*
   • R55 (Shorts): <pending review count> awaiting review
   • R57 (images): <X scheduled today>, <Y posted, Z pending>
   • R61 (videos): <X scheduled today>, <gate count> gates pending
   • Blotato hold: ACTIVE (schedule-only)
5. If any gate is pending, list it with a clear approve/redo option.

crons.name: Keep-alive ping every 6 hours
crons.prompt: Internal keep-alive only. Verify the Telegram channel is still connected by calling getMe via the channel. Do NOT send a Telegram message to the user. If the connection is broken, log it to shared/memory/convo_log_primary.md and try once to reinitialize.

crons.name: R61 human-gate notifier (15 min poll)
crons.prompt:
Check shared/gates/pending.json. For every entry in gates[] with status="pending" AND notified=false, send a Telegram message in this format:
*R61 Gate <gate_number> — <gate_name>*
• Record: <record_id>
• Ad: <ad_name>
• Asset: <video_url> (omit line if empty)
• Next: <next_step from the extra fields, if present>

Reply with: *approve <record_id>*, *redo <record_id> <reason>*, or *hold*.

After sending, set notified=true for that entry and save the file atomically. If the file is missing or gates[] is empty, do nothing — silent is correct.
